description: In a softly lit coffee shop, Rachel, Monica, and Phoebe sit on the left side of a seating area, facing Ross, Chandler, and Joey on the right. Ambient chatter and soft background music are audible.
Monica gestures with her left hand as the group sits together.
Monica says, "What you guys don't understand is, for us, kissing is as important as any part of it." She points a finger forward.
Ross nods slightly and says, "Right."
Joey leans forward, turning his head toward the women with a slight smile.
Joey asks, "Serious?"
Phoebe nods rapidly in response.
Phoebe says, "Oh, yeah." Rachel says, "Everything you need to know is in that first kiss." Monica adds, "Absolutely."
Chandler gestures with his right hand and says, "Yeah, I think for us, kissing is pretty much like an opening act, you know? I mean, it's like the stand up comedian you have to sit through"
Chandler raises both hands and finishes, "before Pink Floyd comes out." Ross nods in agreement.
The women sit quietly. Rachel crosses her arms over her chest.
Ross adjusts a small object in his hands and says, "Yeah, and it's not that we don't like the comedian. It's just that that's not why we bought the"
Ross says, "ticket."
Chandler points at Ross, nodding in agreement.
Chandler gestures with his right index finger and says, "You see, the problem is, though, after the concert's over, no matter how great the show was, you girls are always looking"
Chandler gestures widely with both hands, continuing, "for the comedian again."
Ross raises his hands slightly.
Ross says, "We're in the car, we're fighting traffic."
Chandler raises his hands again and says, "Basically just trying to stay awake."
Rachel looks toward Chandler, her arms still crossed.
Rachel leans forward and says, "Yeah, well, word of advice bring back the comedian."
Rachel gestures briefly with her hand and says, "Otherwise, next time, you're going to find yourself sitting at home listening to that album alone." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey sits back, looking between the others with a still expression. The audience laughter continues and subsides.
Joey asks, "Are we still talking about sex?" Audience laughter rises again.
Ross rests his face in his hand. The screen goes black.
Upbeat instrumental music plays. Outside a building, a sign reads "MUSEUM OF PREHISTORIC HISTORY."
Inside an exhibit room, Ross walks past a cave-person display alongside a woman in a red vest.
Ross looks at the display and says, "No, it's good. It it is good. It's just that doesn't she seem a little angry?"
The woman in the red vest says, "Well, she has issues." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks at her.
Ross asks, "Does she?" The woman gestures toward the display and says, "Trying to live with Mr. 'Hey, I'm evolving.'"
The woman extends her hand toward the male cave-person figure.
The woman says, "Banging other women over the head with a club while she sits at home trying to get the mastodon smell out of the carpet."
Ross raises his hands with palms open.
Ross raises both hands with open palms and says, "Marsha, these are cave people, okay?"
Ross raises a fist and says, "They have issues. Like, gee, that glacier is getting kind of close." Audience laughter is audible.
Marsha points a finger forward.
Marsha says, "See? Speaking of issues, isn't that your ex wife?"
Ross turns his head to look where Marsha pointed.
Ross says, "No. Yes, it is. Carol, hi." Outside the exhibit glass, a blonde woman waves.
Ross waves his hand in return.
Ross says, "Yes, it is. How about I'll catch up with you in the Ice Age?" Marsha asks, "Can I stay?" Ross points his finger and says, "That would be no." Audience laughter is audible.
Marsha walks away. Ross turns to face the display and touches the club held by the male cave-person figure.
The display arm holding the club detaches and falls to the sandy floor. Ross bends down to retrieve it.
Ross stands holding the detached fake arm as Carol approaches him.
Carol says, "Hi," and embraces Ross. Ross holds the fake arm outward while returning the hug with his other arm.
Ross steps back and places the fake arm onto the display. Carol stands nearby.
Carol asks, "Is this a bad time?" Ross gestures to the display and says, "No, it's it's it's the Stone Age."
Ross places his hands on his hips. Audience laughter is audible.
Carol says, "You look great."
Ross lowers his gaze slightly and says, "I I hate that."
Carol looks downward.
Carol says, "Sorry."
Ross says, "Thanks. You look good, too. Well, in here, anyone who"
Ross gestures with his hand toward the space around them.
Ross finishes, "stands a wreck." Audience laughter is audible.
Carol smiles and lets out a soft laugh. Ross looks at her.
In a museum exhibit with artificial plants and a caveman display, Ross stands facing a blonde woman, Carol.
Ross gestures with both hands. "So, what's new? Still a lesbian? Well," he asks. Carol shakes her head slightly.
Ross drops his hands.
"you never know," Ross adds, shrugging.
Ross and Carol stand looking at each other.
Ross asks, "How's the family?" Carol replies, "Marty's still totally paranoid. Oh, and"
Ross interrupts, asking, "why are you here, Carol?"
Carol looks down and then back at Ross.
Carol says, "I'm pregnant."
Ross stares at her and asks, "Pregnant?"
Ross stands motionless with his mouth slightly open. Audience laughter is audible.
A television screen shows a blonde woman in a plaid shirt with her hands on her hips.

On the TV, the woman says, "Looks like she didn't leave in such a hurry after all." The setting changes to an apartment living room. Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe sit on armchairs and a sofa. Chandler points at the TV and says, "Oh, I think this is the episode of Three's Company where there's some kind of"
Chandler finishes, "misunderstanding."
Phoebe turns her head toward Chandler.
Phoebe raises both hands and says, "Then I've already seen this one." She lowers her hands.
Monica takes a spray bottle and cloth from a table and walks over to Joey.
Monica takes a water bottle from Joey's hand and asks, "Are you through with that?" Joey gestures and replies, "Yeah, sorry. The swallowing slowed me down." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica picks up a small item from the coffee table.
"Whose little ball of paper is this?" Monica asks. Chandler points to himself and says, "Oh, that would be mine. See, I wrote a note to myself, and then I realized I"
Chandler lowers his hand.
Chandler finishes, "didn't need the note, so I balled it up, and now I wish I was dead." Monica walks behind the sofa, fluffing a pillow.
Monica continues fluffing pillows on the sofa.
Phoebe says, "She already flipped that pillow. Monica, you know, you already flipped, but it's fine." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica stops and looks at Phoebe.
Monica says, "Look, I'm sorry, guys."
Monica makes quick hand gestures. "I just don't want to give them any more ammunition than they already have," she says. Chandler replies, "Yes, and we all know how cruel a parent"
Chandler gestures forward and finishes, "can be about the flatness of a child's pillow." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey stands up and walks toward Monica.
Joey places a hand on Monica's arm and says, "Would you relax? You do this every time they come. The place looks great."
Joey gestures toward the stove and continues, "You got a beautiful lasagna here that looks good enough to"
Joey waves his hand over the stove and finishes, "avoid touching."
Monica continues moving around the kitchen, grabbing items. Joey walks back to the living area.
Phoebe leans forward on the sofa and says, "Monica. Hi."
Monica turns to look at Phoebe.
Phoebe says, "Monica, you're scaring me."
Monica stares at Phoebe.
Phoebe raises her hands and says, "I mean, you're, like you're, like, all chaotic and and not in a good way." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica stands still.
Joey says, "Calm down. You don't see Ross getting all chaotic and twirly every time they come."
Monica gestures with a cloth and replies, "That's because as far as my parents are concerned,"
Monica turns and grabs an orange, continuing, "Ross can do no wrong."
Monica gestures with the orange and says, "You see, he's the prince. Apparently, they had some big ceremony before I was born." She sets the orange down.
Chandler stands up abruptly and walks toward the large window.
Looking out the window, Chandler points and speaks. An off-screen voice asks, "What?" Chandler says, "Ugly Naked Guy got a thigh master." Audience laughter is audible.
The group reacts, making facial expressions of disgust. Rachel opens the front door and walks in.
Rachel holds her hands near her chest. "Has anybody seen my engagement ring?" she asks. Phoebe smiles and replies, "Yeah, it's beautiful."
Rachel stands still with her mouth open and eyes wide. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel quickly searches around the living room, lifting sofa cushions.
Rachel drops to her knees and looks under the coffee table. Monica, Joey, and Chandler watch her.
Rachel says, "Oh, like I wasn't dreading tomorrow enough having to give it back to him."
Rachel gestures with one hand, saying, "Hi, Barry. Remember me?"
Rachel continues, "I'm the girl in the veil that stomped on your heart in front of your entire family. Oh, God."
Rachel leans back, gripping a cushion. "And now I'm gonna have to return the ring without the ring."
Rachel leans her head back and says loudly, "Which makes it so much harder." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica approaches Rachel.
Monica leans down and says, "Easy, Rachel. We'll find it. Won't wait." Joey and Chandler stand up.
Monica kneels beside Rachel and continues, "Okay, look, it's going to be okay. You're going to give it back to him. It'll all"
Monica finishes, "be over, and we'll eat a lot of ice."
Rachel says, her voice strained, "Okay. Okay. It's a little pear shaped diamond." Monica says, "I'll tell you what."
Monica gestures with one finger and says, "Any diamond ring we find, we'll run it by you."
Rachel returns to searching under the cushions.
Joey asks, "All right, when'd you have it on last". Chandler adds, "doy. Probably right before she lost it."
Chandler looks at Joey. Joey stares back at him.
In a living room, Phoebe and Rachel search around a sofa. Chandler, wearing a patterned shirt, stands near the kitchen counter. Chandler says, "You don't get a lot of doy these days."
Rachel turns away from the sofa. Chandler walks into the kitchen area.
Rachel gestures as she speaks. "No, I had it this morning."
Rachel lowers her hands.
Phoebe turns and points with one finger. "I know. I had it when I was in the kitchen with Dinah," she says.
In the kitchen, Monica searches through cabinets. Rachel approaches her.
Rachel touches Monica's arm. "Oh, don't be mad," Rachel says.
Monica turns to face Rachel.
Monica says, "You didn't." Rachel replies, "Oh, I'm sorry." Monica pulls a glass baking dish of lasagna from an upper shelf. "I gave you one job," Monica says.
Monica raises the dish.
Monica lifts the lasagna dish above her head, looking at the bottom. Rachel points up at the dish and says, "Oh, but look how straight those noodles are."
Rachel lowers her hand.
Chandler stands nearby, gesturing toward Monica. "Monica, you know that's not how you look for an engagement ring in a lasagna," he says. Joey stands beside him.
Monica lowers the dish, sets it on the table, and rubs her forehead.
Monica shakes her head and says, "I just can't do it."
Joey steps toward the table.
Joey claps his hands together. "Boys," he says.
Joey gestures toward the table.
Joey says, "we're going in."
Joey, Chandler, and Phoebe lean over the table, digging their fingers into the lasagna.
Monica walks quickly away from the table toward the door.
Monica opens the front door. Ross stands in the hallway, wearing a suit and tie, holding a folded umbrella. He exhales audibly.
Monica looks at him and says, "That is not a happy high."
Ross looks at Monica.
Ross steps inside. "Carol's pregnant," he says.
At the table, Phoebe holds up a small object. "Oh, I found it," she says.
Monica closes the door. Ross stands near the doorway, holding his umbrella.
Monica approaches Ross and gestures with her hands as he looks downward.
Ross says, "Yeah."
Ross points his finger toward Phoebe and Monica.
Ross says, "Do that for another 2 hours, you might be where I am right about now."
Chandler looks toward Ross.
Chandler stands up from the table. "Kind of puts that whole pillow thing in perspective, huh, man?" he says, gesturing toward Ross.
Monica steps behind Ross and places a hand on his back.
"Well, now, how do you fit into this whole thing?" Monica asks.
Ross gestures with his hands. "Well, Carol says she and Susan want me to be involved. But if I'm not comfortable with"
Ross continues, "it, I don't have to be involved," lowering his hands.
"Basically, it's totally up to me," Ross says. Phoebe looks toward him. "She is so great. I miss her," she says.
Ross turns his head to look at Phoebe. Monica continues standing behind Ross, keeping her hand on his back.
Monica asks, "What does she mean by involved?" Chandler points a finger. "I mean, presumably the biggest part of your job is done"
Joey looks toward Ross.
Joey smiles and adds, "and the most enjoyable."
Rachel turns her head toward Joey, maintaining a flat facial expression.
Rachel looks toward Phoebe. "Phoebe, say something," she says.
Ross shifts his posture on the sofa arm.
Ross sits on the arm of the sofa, gesturing. "Anyway, they want me to go down to this sonogram thing with them tomorrow," he says.
"Wow," Rachel says. Chandler says, "Yeah. Remember back when life was simpler and she was just a lesbian?"
Ross sits with his head lowered and hands clasped.
Ross says softly, "Those were the days."
Rachel looks at Ross.
Rachel asks, "So, what are you going to do?"
Ross raises his head and opens his hands. "I have no idea. No matter what I do, though, I'm still going to be a father." Monica rubs his shoulder.
Ross looks downward again. Joey sits at the kitchen table, using a fork to eat the lasagna.
Joey continues eating from the glass dish.
Phoebe, Chandler, Ross, Monica, and Rachel watch Joey from the living room area.
Joey speaks with food in his mouth, saying, "Righteous."
The setting changes to a city street at night, with cars driving past illuminated storefronts.
The setting changes to a different apartment. Judy sits on a sofa. Monica serves appetizers from a tray. Judy says, "Oh, Martha Ludwin's daughter is going to call you."
Judy picks up an appetizer and examines it closely. Jack sits in an armchair nearby.
Judy takes a bite and asks, "What's that curry taste?"
Monica walks past the sofa. "Curry," she replies.
Judy nods and finishes the bite of the appetizer.
Ross approaches Jack and hands him a glass of wine.
Ross sits on the arm of the sofa. "I think they're great," he says.
Ross nods and says, "I really do."
Jack looks at Ross. "You remember the Ludwins? The big one? Had a thing for you, didn't she?"
Judy waves her hand. "They all had a thing for him."
Monica turns around in the kitchen area.
"I'm sorry. Why is this girl going to call me?" Monica asks.
Judy gestures. "Oh, she just graduated and she wants to be something in cooking or food or"
"I don't know. Anyway, I told her you have a restaurant," Judy says.
Monica raises her hands. "No, Mom, I don't have a restaurant. I work in a restaurant." Judy replies, "Well, they don't have to"
"know that," Judy finishes.
Ross looks forward with a neutral facial expression.
In a brightly lit apartment, Monica stands in the kitchen area preparing food. In the background living area, Jack and Judy sit on couches while Ross stands nearby.
Monica looks toward the living area. "Could you come and help me with spaghetti, please?"
Ross walks toward the kitchen, saying, "Yes," and Judy, holding a blue glass, says, "we're having spaghetti."
Ross walks past Jack and Judy toward the kitchen.
Judy smiles and raises her glass. "That's easy."
Ross approaches Monica in the kitchen.

Monica speaks softly to Ross. "Actually, we were going to have lasagna." In the living room, Jack says, "Oh, I love lasagna." Monica says, "Well, we're not having it." Judy says, "Why do you bring it up? You know how"
Judy continues, "he latches on."
Monica turns back to Ross, gesturing with her hands.
Monica's volume is softer than her previous line. "I know this is going to sound unbelievably selfish on my part, but were you planning on bringing up the whole baby lesbian thing?"
Monica gestures toward herself. "Because I think it might take some of the heat off me."
The setting changes to a dining table where Ross, Monica, Jack, and Judy sit eating spaghetti.
Jack takes a bite of food and says, "what that Rachel did to her life. We ran into her parents at the club. They were not playing very well."
Jack gestures with his fork. "I'm not going to tell you what they spent on that wedding, but $40,000 is a lot of money."
Ross looks at Jack. Monica continues to eat her spaghetti.
Monica stops eating and looks down. "Well, at least she had the chance to leave a man at the altar."
Monica presses her lips together, keeping her gaze forward.
Judy leans forward. "What's that supposed to mean?"
Monica looks at Judy. "Nothing."
Monica shakes her head slightly, her lips still pressed together.
Monica says, "It's an expression." Judy replies, "No, it's not." Jack gestures with a piece of bread. "Don't listen to your mother. You're independent and you always have been. Even"
Jack points his piece of bread toward Monica. "when you were a kid and you were chubby and you had no friends. You were just fine."
Monica looks down. Jack continues eating.
Jack gestures with his bread again. "And you would read alone in your room and your puzzles."
Jack takes a bite of bread. Monica watches him, her lips pressed together.
Jack raises his blue glass. "Look, there are people like Ross who need to shoot for the stars with his museum and his papers getting published."
Jack points toward the table. "Other people are satisfied with staying where they are. I'm telling you, these are the people who never get cancer."
Monica keeps her gaze fixed on Jack.
Jack raises his glass again. "They're happy with what they have. They're basically content."
Jack gestures with his free hand. "Like cows."
Ross looks up from his plate.
Ross's pitch rises on the first word as he looks at Jack. "Cows? Dad, she knows how much I love cows." He then looks at Monica.
Jack uses a piece of bread to wipe his plate and picks it up.
Jack holds the food. "And I read about these women trying to have it all, and I thank God our little Harmonica doesn't seem to have that."
Monica rests her cheek on her hand.
Jack gestures with the food toward Monica. "I'm telling you, sweetheart, you're going to be fine."
Monica keeps her hand on her face. "Thank you, Daddy."
Judy stands in the kitchen area holding a metal splatter screen.
Judy holds up the metal splatter screen, examining it. "Oh, so this does work."
At the table, Monica rubs her temples with both hands, then turns toward Ross.
Monica rests her chin on her hands, looking at Ross. "So, Ross, what's going on with you?"
Ross looks at Monica.
Monica leans closer to Ross. "Any stories? No news? No little anecdotes to share with the folks?"
Ross sighs audibly, pushes back his chair, and stands up.
Ross stands near the table, his hands at his waist. "Look, I realize you guys have been wondering what exactly happened between Carol and me,"
Ross looks around at his parents. "and so well, here's the deal."
Ross lowers his hands.
Ross says, "Carol's a lesbian."
Judy's eyes widen as she touches her collarbone.
Ross looks downward slightly. "She's living with a woman named Susan."
Jack looks up toward Ross.
Ross looks at Jack. "She's pregnant with my child."
Ross pauses, looking at his parents.
Ross gestures with his hands outward. "And she and Susan are going to raise the baby."
Judy keeps her gaze directed upward, her lips slightly parted.
The screen turns completely black.
